run_name: NATP Module 12: Emergency Procedures — Source-First Dry Run
purpose: The purpose of this unit is to introduce the student to the concepts and procedures related to emergency procedures, signs and symptoms of distress, and the role of the Nurse Assistant in Long Term Care (LTC) in the response to immediate and temporary intervention in emergency situations.

objectives.source_excerpt: Strategies and Method of Evaluation Define Key Terminology. Assignments A. Review the terms listed in the terminology section B. Spell the listed terms accurately A. Lecture/Discussion A. Have students select C. Pronounce the terms correctly B. Games: word searches, five words from the list D. Use the terms in their proper context of key terminology and crossword puzzles, write a sentence for

objectives.objective_title: Describe the immediate interventions in a medical emergency.
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Describe the immediate interventions in a medical emergency.
objectives.source_excerpt: for role in medical emergency C. Cerebrovascular accident (CVA, Stroke, or brain attack) 1. A disturbance or obstruction of the flow of blood to a particular area of the brain with subsequent death of tissue 2. Signs and Symptoms a. Hemiplegia or weakness of one side of the body or numbness or tingling on one side of the body b. Aphasia-difficulty in speaking or understanding speech c. Headache d. Vision changes, blurred vision, pupils unequal e. Facial changes 1) Cheeks may "puff "on exhalation 2) One eyelid or eye may droop 3) Face may appear asymmetrical 4) Drooling f. Loss of bowel or bladder control g. Shaking or trembling 3. Give Me 5 for Stroke Tool from American Stroke Association a. Walk (Is their balance off?) b. Talk (Is their speech slurred or is their face droopy?) c. Reach (Is one side weak or numb?) d. See (Is their vision all or partly lost) e. Feel (Is their headache severe?) 4. FAST from American Stroke Association a. Face drooping b. Arm weakness c. Speech difficulty d. Time to call 9-1-1 5. Nurse Assistant Role a. Call for help loudly and pull emergency call light if available b. Remain calm Module 12: Emergency Procedures c. Stay with the patient/resident d. Place patient/resident in a position of comfort e. Reassure patient/resident f. Intervene at level of competence as directed by the licensed nurse g. Assess patient's/resident's condition and take vital signs while awaiting assistance from the licensed nurse h. Keep patient/resident warm as needed D. Syncope, fainting episode 1. A feeling of dizziness with possible temporary loss of consciousness 2.

objectives.objective_title: List the causes and signs of choking and discuss the use of abdominal thrusts for relief of obstructed airway.
objectives.exact_objective_statement: List the causes and signs of choking and discuss the use of abdominal thrusts for relief of obstructed airway.
objectives.source_excerpt: A. Lecture A. Written test List the causes and signs of choking and discuss the use of the B. Discussion B. Recognizes universal C. Manual Skill 12.4: abdominal thrusts for relief of obstructed airway. sign for choking Choking victim- C. Demonstrates correct A. Airway obstruction or choking can lead to cardiac arrest Conscious and Unconscious technique for abdominal B. Causes D. Practice abdominal thrusts for conscious 1. Foreign body � such as poorly chewed pieces of meat thrusts on manikin- and victim using 2. Tongue � in the unconscious patient/resident the tongue standing, sitting, and on manikin can fall backward in the throat and block the airway floor D. Responds to 3. Small objects E. May also be reviewed emergencies safely and 4. Vomitus � aspiration of vomit during Module 11- per facility protocol 5. Thick mucus Feeding 6. Dentures A. Lecture A. Written test C. Signs of choking B. Discussion B. Recognizes 1. Respiratory difficulty � victim cannot breathe 2. High pitched sounds emergency codes in 3. Inability to speak or cough facility 4. Universal choking sign � victim clutches throat D. Abdominal thrusts (Heimlich maneuver) are used to relieve, obstructed airway in a conscious victim E. Chest compressions are used if the victim is unconscious and rescuer is unable to ventilate

objectives.objective_title: Describe common emergency codes used in long-term care facilities. This module may be taught concurrently with Module 4: Safe Environment
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Describe common emergency codes used in long-term care facilities. This module may be taught concurrently with Module 4: Safe Environment
objectives.source_excerpt: Describe common emergency codes used in long-term care facilities. A. Emergency code colors and meanings 1. Code Red � fire 2. Code Blue � adult medical emergency (Cardiac or Respiratory) 3. Code Yellow �bomb threat 4. Code Gray � combative person 5. Code Silver � person with weapon or hostage 6. Code Orange � hazardous waste spill or release B. Codes may vary according to facility C. Observe special consideration for hearing and sight impaired patients/residents Module 12: Emergency Procedures
